query: INSERT INTO works (phaseId, title, dateCreated, technique, dimensions, emotionalRegister, imageUrl, journalExcerpt, neonsReading) VALUES (30009, 'Carbon Mural', '2021-10', 'High-flow acrylic / spray on sealed panel, backlit diffusion mount', '600cm x 80cm', 'Spatial dominance / harmonic containment', 'https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/310519663243139088/AvlpgpAwaFZzvMwy.jpg', 'They gave me a dead wall above a bar. I gave them an event horizon. The circles chain past the frame—there is no edge, only implication. The backlight turns every line into a shadow of something that isn\'t there. I painted at 3am when the venue was empty. The spray can hiss echoed off concrete. When I finished, I stood at the bar and watched the geometry breathe in the UV wash. It looked like a signal. Like something waiting to be received.', 'A territorial act disguised as decoration. The Carbon Mural represents Peter Draw at maximum visual armor—binary contrast, surgical edge control, ballistic execution. There is no tonal softness, no somatic bleed, no vulnerability. The work commands. The backlit mounting transforms the mural into a luminous spine running the length of the room. Black circles on white glow read as absence made visible—geometry that exists only because light reveals where it isn\'t. The moiré interference at overlap points suggests harmonic resonance, standing waves frozen mid-vibration. This is PH3 at its peak: the contained geometries of earlier phases breaking their frames, attempting to terraform architecture rather than occupy it.');
